title: Relevance scoring configuration
description: Controls how papers are scored for relevance. Keywords are organised into named profiles, each with a focus multiplier. Author name substrings can receive bonus points. The effective weight for a keyword is its base weight multiplied by its profile's focus value.

titleMultiplier.description: Weight multiplier applied to keyword hits in the paper title vs the abstract. Default 2.0 means a title match is worth twice an abstract match.

minScoreToInclude.description: Papers whose final relevance_score is below this floor are dropped by rank_papers(). digest.py's minRelevanceScore acts as a secondary filter on top.

authorBoosts.description: Map of author name substrings (case-insensitive) to bonus point values added to the paper score when any author matches. Example: { "de Leon": 5, "Oliver": 4 }.

profiles.description: Named keyword groups. Each profile has a focus multiplier (0 = silent) and a keywords map (keyword string → integer weight, negative weights penalise).

focus.description: Multiplier applied to all keyword weights in this profile. Set to 0 to silence the entire profile.

keywords.description: Keyword string → integer weight. Negative weights penalise papers. Title hits are multiplied by titleMultiplier.
